# ZLAR

> ZLAR is where AI action becomes answerable.

ZLAR keeps humans in the loop when routed AI actions start doing real things. Before a governed action acts, ZLAR checks the rule. The rule can allow it, block it, or ask a person. A log records what happened. A ZLAR receipt records what counted as authorized effect when action tried to become consequence.

Primary pages:

- [Home](https://zlar.ai/): the doorway thesis, operator routing, sample receipt, scope disclosure, and contact.
- [Enterprise](https://zlar.ai/enterprise.html): rules before enterprise AI changes files, calls tools, or starts workflows.
- [Government](https://zlar.ai/government.html): records for public AI actions: what happened, what rule applied, and what proof remains.
- [Financial Services](https://zlar.ai/financial-services.html): the gap between login and what AI is allowed to do.
- [Healthcare](https://zlar.ai/healthcare.html): bounded healthcare workflow framing without clinical claims.
- [Defense / Military](https://zlar.ai/defense.html): command visibility, rules, people, and proof for routed actions.
- [Founder Note](https://zlar.ai/founder-note.html): first-person statement of the threshold thesis.
- [Sample receipt](https://zlar.ai/proof-pack.html): human-first sample receipt with optional bounded terminal verification, manifest, coverage report, hashes, verifier, PASS output, and explicit non-claims.
- [Release metadata](https://zlar.ai/release.json): machine-readable current-release pointer for direct live-site verification.
- [Open Memo on Action-Level Assurance](https://zlar.ai/open-memo.html): policy memo for rules before action and receipts afterward.
- [Boundaries and Non-Claims](https://zlar.ai/boundaries.html): what passes through ZLAR, what does not, and what the sample does not prove.
- [Archive](https://zlar.ai/archive.html): affected-person pathway, Sealed Mode, older proof pages, standards submissions, specs, legal, and earlier founder writing.

Claim boundary:

- Current public release: ZLAR v3.4.32 - Release-asset readiness handoff.
- ZLAR governs routed/intercepted action surfaces only.
- Safe Codex wording: "ZLAR can govern Codex CLI-invoked MCP tool calls when those MCP servers are routed through ZLAR."
- Unrouted shell/filesystem/browser/app/network/model-reasoning/final-text surfaces are not claimed as governed by the current proof path.
- /contest is not implemented.
- A private-by-default non-Vincent verifier request has been sent; no public external attestation is claimed in this repo, and any private reply or later result remains bounded by verifier relationship, disclosure permission, and exact evidence returned.

Interpretation rule:

Founder and audience pages explain the thesis. Disclosure boxes and boundary pages carry the precise public claim. If a page appears to imply broader coverage than the boundary page, read the boundary page as controlling.
